cases.description: Explicit scalar return of an arithmetic expression over args; no locals, no DECLARE. Confirms args pass through by name and SQL operator precedence is untouched.
cases.ruby_create:
CREATE FUNCTION plx_add(a int, b int) RETURNS int
LANGUAGE plxruby AS $$
return a + b * 2
$$;

cases.description: String interpolation in a returned value: local inferred text (from string literal), interpolation lowered to || with ::text cast, trailing literal chunk preserved. RHS is not a constant literal so no init-fold.
cases.ruby_create:
CREATE FUNCTION plx_greet(name text) RETURNS text
LANGUAGE plxruby AS $$
greeting = "Hello, #{name}!"
return greeting
$$;

cases.description: if/elsif/else chain with explicit text annotation on a branch-assigned local (declared once, no fold). >= comparisons pass through; openers end in THEN, closer END IF.
cases.ruby_create:
CREATE FUNCTION plx_grade(score int) RETURNS text
LANGUAGE plxruby AS $$
grade #:: text
if score >= 90
  grade = "A"
elsif score >= 80
  grade = "B"
else
  grade = "F"
end
return grade
$$;

cases.description: Block-form unless lowering to IF NOT (cond) with the condition parenthesized; exercises && -> AND and == -> = together. status inferred text and init-folded (top-level, first-reference, string literal).
cases.ruby_create:
CREATE FUNCTION plx_access(active boolean, role text) RETURNS text
LANGUAGE plxruby AS $$
status = "ok"
unless active && role == "admin"
  status = "denied"
end
return status
$$;

cases.description: Modifier unless on a return statement, plus != -> <>. Modifier expands to IF NOT (cond) THEN <stmt>; END IF; no frame pushed.
cases.ruby_create:
CREATE FUNCTION plx_zcheck(n int) RETURNS text
LANGUAGE plxruby AS $$
return "empty" unless n != 0
return "nonzero"
$$;

cases.description: Integer for over 1..n with modifier next-if and break-if lowering to CONTINUE WHEN / EXIT WHEN. Loop var i is plpgsql-implicit (not hoisted); total inferred integer and init-folded.
cases.ruby_create:
CREATE FUNCTION plx_sum_skip(n int) RETURNS int
LANGUAGE plxruby AS $$
total = 0
for i in 1..n
  next if i == 3
  break if i > 7
  total = total + i
end
return total
$$;

cases.description: (LO...HI).each do |j| block form with an EXCLUSIVE range: lowers to FOR j IN LO..(HI - 1) LOOP. s inferred integer and init-folded.
cases.ruby_create:
CREATE FUNCTION plx_exsum(n int) RETURNS int
LANGUAGE plxruby AS $$
s = 0
(0...n).each do |j|
  s = s + j
end
return s
$$;

cases.description: Ternary C ? A : B in a returned expression lowering to CASE WHEN C THEN A ELSE B END; >= comparison passes through.
cases.ruby_create:
CREATE FUNCTION plx_sign(x int) RETURNS text
LANGUAGE plxruby AS $$
return x >= 0 ? "nonneg" : "neg"
$$;

cases.description: raise notice: with interpolation -> RAISE NOTICE with % placeholder and ordered arg; a literal % in the message is escaped to %%. Followed by a scalar return.
cases.ruby_create:
CREATE FUNCTION plx_note(id int) RETURNS int
LANGUAGE plxruby AS $$
raise notice: "processing id #{id} at 50%done"
return id * 10
$$;
cases.expected_output:
NOTICE:  processing id 7 at 50%done
 r  
----
 70
(1 row)

cases.description: Conditional raise exception: with interpolation and an errcode: option -> RAISE EXCEPTION '...' , arg USING ERRCODE = '...'. Test drives the failing path so psql prints the ERROR.
cases.ruby_create:
CREATE FUNCTION plx_checkpos(v int) RETURNS int
LANGUAGE plxruby AS $$
if v < 0
  raise exception: "negative: #{v}", errcode: "22023"
end
return v
$$;
cases.expected_output:
ERROR:  negative: -3
CONTEXT:  PL/pgSQL function plx_checkpos(integer) line 4 at RAISE

cases.description: Explicit numeric type annotations: trailing `#:: numeric` on a first constant assignment folds into the DECLARE init; standalone `amount #:: numeric` declares without init (RHS is an expression). Return interpolates the numeric via ::text.
cases.ruby_create:
CREATE FUNCTION plx_interest(principal numeric) RETURNS text
LANGUAGE plxruby AS $$
rate = 0.05 #:: numeric
amount #:: numeric
amount = principal * rate
return "interest: #{amount}"
$$;

cases.description: Literal type inference across three locals (string->text, int->integer, true/false->boolean), each top-level/first-reference/constant so all init-fold into the DECLARE. Return builds a multi-interpolation string; a leading empty chunk drops (lone #{label} -> (label)::text).
cases.ruby_create:
CREATE FUNCTION plx_fmt() RETURNS text
LANGUAGE plxruby AS $$
label = "count"
n = 42
active = true
return "#{label}=#{n} active=#{active}"
$$;

cases.description: until loop -> WHILE NOT (cond) LOOP with the condition parenthesized; two locals inferred integer and init-folded.
cases.ruby_create:
CREATE FUNCTION plx_until_sum(n int) RETURNS int
LANGUAGE plxruby AS $$
i = 0
count = 0
until i >= n
  count = count + i
  i = i + 1
end
return count
$$;

cases.description: SETOF via RETURNS TABLE + emit -> RETURN NEXT. TABLE columns idx/label are params (assigned with :=, never hoisted); integer for var i is implicit. No DECLARE section is emitted.
cases.ruby_create:
CREATE FUNCTION plx_rows(n int) RETURNS TABLE(idx int, label text)
LANGUAGE plxruby AS $$
for i in 1..n
  idx = i
  label = "row#{i}"
  emit
end
$$;
cases.expected_output:
idx | label 
-----+-------
   1 | row1
   2 | row2
   3 | row3
(3 rows)

cases.description: begin/rescue => e without ensure -> nested BEGIN/EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS; e.message rewrites to SQLERRM. Division by zero is caught, a notice raised, and a fallback value returned.
cases.ruby_create:
CREATE FUNCTION plx_safediv(d int) RETURNS int
LANGUAGE plxruby AS $$
begin
  return 100 / d
rescue => e
  raise notice: "caught: #{e.message}"
  return -1
end
$$;
cases.expected_output:
NOTICE:  caught: division by zero
 r  
----
 -1
(1 row)

cases.description: query(...).each do |row| iterating a static SELECT, summing a record field into a hoisted local; static interpolation #{g} lowers to a bare name reference.
cases.ruby_create:
CREATE FUNCTION c1(g int) RETURNS bigint LANGUAGE plxruby AS $$
total = 0  #:: bigint
query("SELECT amount FROM orders WHERE grp = #{g}").each do |row|
  total = total + row.amount
end
return total
$$;

cases.description: row = fetch_one(SELECT) lowers to SELECT * INTO <record> FROM (<user sql>) AS __plx_fo_1; whole-row target hoists RECORD; field access after.
cases.ruby_create:
CREATE FUNCTION c2(uid int) RETURNS text LANGUAGE plxruby AS $$
u = fetch_one("SELECT id, name FROM users WHERE id = #{uid}")
return u.name
$$;

cases.description: perform(...) with a static DML string is emitted verbatim; perform of a row-returning SELECT becomes PERFORM * FROM (<sql>) AS __plx_p_1; result discarded.
cases.ruby_create:
CREATE FUNCTION c4(cid int) RETURNS void LANGUAGE plxruby AS $$
perform("UPDATE counters SET n = n + 1 WHERE id = #{cid}")
perform("SELECT count(*) FROM counters")
return
$$;

cases.description: execute(expr, bind) is always dynamic; interpolation #{tbl} becomes runtime string concatenation || (tbl)::text and the extra arg becomes USING.
cases.ruby_create:
CREATE FUNCTION c5(tbl text, note text) RETURNS void LANGUAGE plxruby AS $$
execute("INSERT INTO #{tbl}(msg) VALUES ($1)", note)
return
$$;

cases.description: RETURNS TABLE(cust_id,total) with query iteration; TABLE columns are params (not hoisted), emit lowers to RETURN NEXT.
cases.ruby_create:
CREATE FUNCTION c6() RETURNS TABLE(cust_id int, total bigint) LANGUAGE plxruby AS $$
query("SELECT id, amount FROM orders WHERE grp = 1 ORDER BY id").each do |row|
  cust_id = row.id
  total = row.amount * 2
  emit
end
$$;

cases.description: RETURNS SETOF int with an integer for-loop; return_next e lowers to RETURN NEXT e; trailing bare return -> RETURN;.
cases.ruby_create:
CREATE FUNCTION c7(n int) RETURNS SETOF int LANGUAGE plxruby AS $$
for i in 1..n
  return_next i * i
end
return
$$;

cases.description: return_query(static SELECT) lowers to RETURN QUERY <sql> in a SETOF function.
Record field access via row[:id] and row['name'] both normalize to row.id / row.name; raise notice interpolation -> RAISE NOTICE '%'.
cases.ruby_create:
CREATE FUNCTION c9() RETURNS void LANGUAGE plxruby AS $$
query("SELECT id, name FROM users ORDER BY id").each do |r|
  raise notice: "user #{r[:id]}: #{r['name']}"
end
return
$$;
cases.expected_output:
NOTICE:  user 1: Alice
NOTICE:  user 2: Bob
NOTICE:  user 3: Carol
 c9 
----
 
(1 row)

cases.description: begin/rescue with a Ruby exception class mapping (PG::UniqueViolation -> unique_violation) and e.message -> SQLERRM; local first assigned inside begin still hoisted to outer DECLARE (no init fold, := kept).
cases.ruby_create:
CREATE FUNCTION c10(k int) RETURNS boolean LANGUAGE plxruby AS $$
begin
  execute("INSERT INTO uniq(id) VALUES ($1)", k)
  ok = true  #:: boolean
rescue PG::UniqueViolation => e
  ok = false
  raise notice: "dup #{k}: #{e.message}"
end
return ok
$$;
cases.expected_output:
NOTICE:  dup 1: duplicate key value violates unique constraint "uniq_pkey"
 c10 
-----
 f
(1 row)

 c10 
-----
 t
(1 row)

cases.description: begin/rescue/ensure lowers to the double-nested BEGIN/EXCEPTION pattern so the ensure body runs exactly once on both the normal/handled and the propagate path.
cases.ruby_create:
CREATE FUNCTION c11(k int) RETURNS void LANGUAGE plxruby AS $$
begin
  perform("INSERT INTO t2 VALUES (#{k})")
rescue => e
  raise notice: "failed: #{e.message}"
ensure
  perform("INSERT INTO log2(msg) VALUES ('done')")
end
return
$$;
cases.expected_output:
c11 
-----
 
(1 row)

 x 
---
 5
(1 row)

 msg  
------
 done
(1 row)

cases.description: Integer for i in 1..n (loop var not hoisted, plpgsql auto-declares it) with modifier next if -> CONTINUE WHEN; top-level literal init folded into DECLARE.
cases.ruby_create:
CREATE FUNCTION c12(n int) RETURNS int LANGUAGE plxruby AS $$
s = 0  #:: int
for i in 1..n
  next if i == 3
  s = s + i
end
return s
$$;

cases.description: if/elsif/else with rewritten operators (&& -> AND, || -> OR, == -> =); conditions are boolean SQL, no truthiness coercion.
cases.ruby_create:
CREATE FUNCTION c13(score int, bonus boolean, override boolean) RETURNS text LANGUAGE plxruby AS $$
if score >= 90 && bonus
  return 'A'
elsif score >= 80 || override
  return 'B'
else
  return 'F'
end
$$;
cases.test_sql: SELECT c13(95,true,false) AS a, c13(70,false,true) AS b, c13(50,false,false) AS c;

cases.description: until C -> WHILE NOT (C) LOOP; compound assign += -> := x + (..); modifier break if -> EXIT WHEN.
cases.ruby_create:
CREATE FUNCTION c14(n int) RETURNS int LANGUAGE plxruby AS $$
step = 0  #:: int
until step >= n
  step += 1
  break if step > 100
end
return step
$$;

cases.description: raise notice: with interpolation -> % placeholders + arg list, literal % escaped to %%; ternary ?: -> CASE WHEN ... END; annotated locals.
cases.ruby_create:
CREATE FUNCTION c15(done int, total int) RETURNS text LANGUAGE plxruby AS $$
pct = done * 100 / total  #:: int
label = done == total ? "complete" : "partial"  #:: text
raise notice: "50% checkpoint at #{pct} (#{label})"
return label
$$;
cases.expected_output:
NOTICE:  50% checkpoint at 30 (partial)
   c15   
---------
 partial
(1 row)

cases.description: .each_with_index hoists idx integer and row RECORD, emits idx := -1 before the FOR and injects idx := idx + 1 as the first body statement (0-based).
cases.ruby_create:
CREATE FUNCTION c16() RETURNS void LANGUAGE plxruby AS $$
query("SELECT name FROM users ORDER BY id").each_with_index do |row, idx|
  raise notice: "#{idx}: #{row.name}"
end
return
$$;
cases.expected_output:
NOTICE:  0: Alice
NOTICE:  1: Bob
NOTICE:  2: Carol
 c16 
-----
 
(1 row)

cases.description: case/when is out of the M3 subset and must be rejected at CREATE time with a FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED ereport (no plpgsql body is produced).
cases.ruby_create:
CREATE FUNCTION e_case(x int) RETURNS text LANGUAGE plxruby AS $$
case x
when 1
  return 'one'
else
  return 'other'
end
$$;
cases.expected_plpgsql_body: -- none: transpile aborts at CREATE time; ereport(ERROR, errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED)); no prosrc rewritten
cases.test_sql:
CREATE FUNCTION e_case(x int) RETURNS text LANGUAGE plxruby AS $$
case x
when 1
  return 'one'
else
  return 'other'
end
$$;
cases.expected_output: ERROR:  plxruby: case/when is not supported (out of the M3 subset)

cases.description: A nested `def` (function definition) anywhere in the body is a hard out-of-subset error rejected at CREATE time.
cases.expected_plpgsql_body: -- none: transpile aborts at CREATE time; ereport(ERROR, errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED)); no prosrc rewritten
cases.expected_output: ERROR:  plxruby: 'def' (nested function definition) is not supported

cases.description: A local whose first assignment RHS is a plain function call (no annotation, no literal/cast/fetch_one) cannot be typed and is rejected at CREATE time naming the var and its line.
cases.ruby_create:
CREATE FUNCTION e_infer(x int) RETURNS text LANGUAGE plxruby AS $$
label = compute_thing(x)
return label
$$;
cases.expected_plpgsql_body: -- none: transpile aborts at CREATE time; ereport(ERROR) demanding a #:: annotation; no prosrc rewritten
cases.test_sql:
CREATE FUNCTION e_infer(x int) RETURNS text LANGUAGE plxruby AS $$
label = compute_thing(x)
return label
$$;
cases.expected_output: ERROR:  plxruby: cannot infer a PostgreSQL type for local variable "label" (first assigned at line 1); add an annotation, e.g. label = ... #:: text
